title: Colombia Vs Jordan Highlights, International Friendly 2026: Jhon Arias Leads The Way In Los Cafeteros' Commanding Win | Outlook India
original_text: ColombiaGoalkeeper: Camilo VargasDefenders: Johan Mojica, Jhon Lucumi, Davinson Sanchez, Daniel MunozMidfielders: Richard Rios, Jefferson Lerma, James RodriguezForwards: Luis Diaz, Jhon Cordoba, Jhon AriasJordanGoalkeeper: Yazeed AbulailaDefenders: Yazan Al Arab, Mohammad Abu Al Nadi, Saed Al-RosanMidfielders: Mohannad Abu Taha, Nizar Al Rashdan, Noor Al Rawabdeh, Ehsan HaddadForwards: Mahmoud Al Mardi, Ali Olwan, Mousa TamariWhen: Sunday, June 7Where: Snapdragon Stadium (San Diego, California)Time: 4:30 AM ISTChannel/streaming: N/A In IndiaWe are now minutes away from kick-off at the Snapdragon Stadium between Colombia and Jordan.First Half beginsColombia 0-0 Jordan 1'Well, well! Jordan with the first piece of action as their player Mohannad Abu Taha hits the bar in the opening exchanges.Colombia 0-0 Jordan 5'Sporting CP forward Luis Suarez sets up Jhon Arias with the former Wolves winger having a pop at the Jordan goal but nothing doing.Colombia 0-0 Jordan 11'James Rodriguez, Luis Diaz, Jefferson Lerma and Daniel Munoz are some of the COL stars that are out on action tonight. Colombia will be some team to beat at this World Cup 2026.Colombia 0-0 Jordan 18'Much will depend on Jordan's most influential player, Mousa Al-Tamari, who plays for Rennes in Ligue 1. The captain will want to lead his side from the front and take Jordan beyond the Group Phase.Colombia 0-0 Jordan 25'Mousa Al Tamari sees his effort from outside the box misses to the left, saved by the Colombian goalie.Colombia 0-0 Jordan 27'We have not seen any piece of action that could make someone stand-up from their seat, such as been the tempo of this game.Colombia 0-0 Jordan 36'Jhon Arias breaks the deadlock with a neat finish as Colombia take the lead right before the HT break.Colombia 1-0 Jordan 41'Gustavo Puerta tries a shot from outside the box but is safely pouched by the Jordan keeper. And that's that for the 1st half. Jhon Arias goal separates the two sides.We are underway for the 2nd half between Colombia and Jordan at the Snapdragon Stadium.He may not have worked at Wolves but Jhon Arias is showing why he's such a hot prospect. The attacker slots in the game's second goal to put the result out of Jordan's reach.Colombia 2-0 Jordan 55'Jordan need a response if they are get back in this game. Colombia are right on top in this match as of now.Colombia 2-0 Jordan 62'Jordan ring the changes as they chase a way back into the match. M. Abualnadi replaces A. Nasib, while A. Al-Azaizah comes on for A. Olwan. With time running out, Jordan are injecting fresh legs and attacking intent in a bid to reduce Colombia's advantage.Colombia 2-0 Jordan 80'Red card! Jordan's night goes from bad to worse as A. Jamous is sent off after a reckless challenge on J. Carrascal. The referee wastes no time reaching for the red, and Jamous storms off the pitch in frustration. Reduced to ten men in the dying moments, Jordan now face an even tougher task as Colombia look to see out the result comfortably.Colombia 2-0 Jordan 90+2'Colombia finish the job in emphatic fashion! A dominant display from Los Cafeteros sees them secure a convincing victory, with Jhon Adolfo Arias stealing the spotlight through a standout all-round performance. Colombia controlled the tempo, pressed with intensity, and made their chances count throughout the contest. The result will boost confidence and optimism among fans as the team continues its preparations for the World Cup, while Jordan are left reflecting on a difficult evening capped by a late red card.
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
